					<description><![CDATA[<p>S. Korea goes All In on AI; new Dresden power fab; Intel expansion; GM secures memory supply; AI power management funding; mature-node foundry capacity; 300mm fab equipment; McKinsey's map of strategic IC supply; security threats; AI burnout.</p>
<p>The post <a href="https://semiengineering.com/chip-industry-week-in-review-145/">Chip Industry Week In Review</a> appeared first on <a href="https://semiengineering.com">Semiconductor Engineering</a>.</p>
]]></description>
										<content:encoded><![CDATA[<h3><strong>Around the world</strong></h3>
<p><strong>South Korea</strong> unveiled a sweeping AI and semiconductor investment drive, planning <a href="https://www.motir.go.kr/kor/article/ATCL3f49a5a8c/171974/view">three mega projects</a> that tie semiconductors, physical AI/robotics, and AI data centers into a single industrial plan, with government support for regional chip clusters, packaging capacity, power, water, sites, and workforce development. Among the new investments:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Samsung</strong> will spend $260B on <a href="https://news.samsung.com/kr/%EC%82%BC%EC%84%B1-%EB%AF%B8%EB%9E%98-%EC%84%B1%EC%9E%A5-%EC%9C%84%ED%95%B4-2655%EC%A1%B0%EC%9B%90-%ED%88%AC%EC%9E%90">new semiconductor fabs</a> in <strong>Gwangju</strong> and $36B for HBM fabs in <strong data-start="608" data-end="630">Cheonan</strong> and <strong data-start="608" data-end="630">Onyang, </strong>plus more buildouts for smartphone displays, next-gen batteries and a <strong>Sejong</strong> substrate line.</li>
<li><strong>SK hynix</strong> outlined a broader AI-memory expansion plan that ties together new front-end, NAND, and advanced-packaging capacity. The company <a href="https://news.skhynix.com/fact-05/">plans to invest</a> ~$714B over the mid- to long-term, including ~$386B for its <a href="https://news.skhynix.com/fact-05/">Yongin Semiconductor Cluster</a>, $64B, for <a href="https://news.skhynix.com/fact-07/">Cheongju</a>, and $260B for a new semiconductor cluster in the southwestern region, where it expects to build two memory fabs.</li>
</ul>
<p>In a big win for <strong>Europe,</strong> <strong>Infineon</strong> opened its $5.7B Smart Power Fab in Dresden, with the plant&#8217;s completion coming several months ahead of schedule. The plant is the world&#8217;s largest factory for intelligent <a href="https://www.infineon.com/press-release/2026/ifxpr202607-117">power semiconductors and analog/mixed-signal</a> technologies and will add 1,000 new jobs to the Silicon Saxony cluster.</p>
<p><img data-recalc-dims="1" fetchpriority="high" decoding="async" class="alignnone wp-image-24278387 size-full" src="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?resize=1790%2C1002&#038;ssl=1" alt="" width="1790" height="1002" srcset="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?w=1790&amp;ssl=1 1790w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?resize=300%2C168&amp;ssl=1 300w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?resize=1024%2C573&amp;ssl=1 1024w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?resize=768%2C430&amp;ssl=1 768w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?resize=1536%2C860&amp;ssl=1 1536w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-02-at-1.43.32-PM.png?resize=600%2C336&amp;ssl=1 600w" sizes="(max-width: 1000px) 100vw, 1000px" /><br />
<strong>Fig.1: Infineon&#8217;s new Smart Power Fab in Dresden</strong></p>
<p>In the <strong>U.S.</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Intel</strong> <strong>Foundry</strong> broke ground on the expansion of its <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/posts/intel-foundry_intelfoundry-activity-7477868646850289666-DFAm?utm_source=share&amp;utm_medium=member_desktop&amp;rcm=ACoAAAAKX2sBO9zjXEupEQUaXA6DCfW2N-YJhz8">mask operations facility</a> at the Bowers Campus in Santa Clara, California.</li>
<li><strong>Micron </strong>and <strong>GM</strong> entered into a strategic customer agreement aimed at strengthening semiconductor and automotive supply chains in the U.S. and <a href="https://investors.micron.com/news-releases/news-release-details/micron-and-general-motors-sign-strategic-agreement-secure-supply">ensuring consistent memory and storage access </a>for GM vehicles.</li>
<li><strong>Air Liquide</strong> will spend over $170M to build two new <a href="https://www.airliquide.com/group/press-releases-news/2026-07-01/air-liquide-invests-over-170m-usd-us-support-sk-hynixs-ambition-build-global-ai-memory-ecosystem">high-purity gas production </a>units in Indiana to support <strong>SK hynix</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>FormFactor</strong> was awarded a $24M grant from the Texas IC fund for its new <a href="https://gov.texas.gov/news/post/governor-abbott-announces-texas-semiconductor-innovation-fund-grant-to-formfactor">probe card site</a>.</li>
<li>To beat the memory crunch, <strong>Apple</strong> <a href="https://www.ft.com/content/d72a25e2-7bde-4aa9-bd8d-0c4f3d6cb2cb?syn-25a6b1a6=1">wants to buy</a> memory chips from China’s <strong>CXMT</strong>, which is blacklisted by the US government, reports FT.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Funding</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Reed Semiconductor</strong> raised $100M for its AI infrastructure <a href="https://www.reedsemi.com/newsroom/">power management</a> solutions.</li>
<li><strong>Etched</strong> raised $800M for its <a href="https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/06/30/3319922/0/en/etched-emerges-from-stealth-with-working-chip-800m-raised-and-over-1b-in-customer-contracts.html">AI chips</a> with low-voltage inference for high-throughput workloads and cluster-scale memory for low latency workloads.</li>
<li><strong>Stathera</strong> raised $55M to scale its GEN2 <a href="https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/stathera-announces-us55-million-series-b-to-scale-silicon-timing-and-accelerate-next-generation-ai-data-center-solutions-302813712.html">silicon timing</a> into mass production.</li>
<li><strong>Oxmiq Labs</strong> raised $35M for its <a href="https://oxmiq.ai/blog-oxmiq-raises-35m-series-a">licensable GPU</a> architecture. The startup is positioning its modular chiplet and software stack as an alternative path for semiconductor companies and AI infrastructure builders that want custom AI compute without building a full GPU program from scratch.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Research</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>U. of Florida</strong> researchers published survey results on challenges, priorities, and standardization needs in <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.22149">advanced packaging and heterogeneous integration</a>, with hybrid bonding at the top of the most challenging.</li>
<li><strong>TU Eindhoven</strong> researchers demonstrated Mega, a <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30039">22nm FD-SOI spiking-neural-network accelerator</a> for edge vision that achieves 0.375 pJ/SOP, a reported 4× improvement over prior SNN accelerator designs.</li>
<li><strong>MIT/FUTUR-IC</strong> reported progress on <a href="https://news.mit.edu/2026/mit-researchers-advance-toward-greater-bandwidth-more-energy-efficient-0630">electronic-photonic integration</a>, including optical coupler devices intended to make it easier and less expensive to connect photonic chips with electronic microchips inside a package. MIT frames the work as an “optical bump” analog to solder bumps, with the goal of supporting higher-bandwidth, lower-energy data movement for co-packaged optics and future microsystems using existing foundry and packaging infrastructure.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Reports</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li>AI-related demand is tightening <a href="https://www.trendforce.com/presscenter/news/20260630-13127.html">mature-node foundry capacity</a>, pushing up prices for 8-inch and 12-inch processes and likely extending increases into 2027, according to <strong>TrendForce</strong>. The firm said average utilization across the top 10 foundries’ 8-inch fabs recovered to 88% in 2026, and foundry prices rose 5% to 15% between the first and second quarters.</li>
<li>Worldwide 300mm fab equipment investment for <a href="https://www.semi.org/en/semi-press-release/semi-projects-300mm-memory-equipment-investment-to-surpass-50-billion-dollars-in-2026">memory</a> will exceed $50B for the first time in 2026, rising 29%, followed by another 11% increase in 2027, says <strong>SEMI</strong>. The forecast is being driven by AI-related demand for HBM, DDR5, and data storage, with DRAM equipment spending expected to reach $37B in 2026 and 3D NAND spending projected at $14B.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>AI/cloud</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Nvidia</strong> introduced a new business model that opens up compute access to AI startups and other players, enabling AI clouds to buy Nvidia infrastructure for AI-native, enterprise, and ISV customers with a <a href="https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-unlocks-ai-compute-at-scale-capital-partners-to-power-ai-infrastructure-buildout/">revenue-sharing and credit-support.</a></li>
<li><strong>Microsoft</strong> launched a <a href="https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/07/02/microsoft-frontier-company-ai-engineering-that-amplifies-and-protects-your-intelligence/">Microsoft Frontier Company</a> to support enterprise AI transformation, with a $2.5B investment and 6,000 industry and engineering experts to co-design, co-innovate, deploy and continuously improve AI systems at scale.</li>
<li><strong>Meta </strong>will develop a cloud business to <a href="https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-01/meta-is-building-a-cloud-business-to-sell-excess-ai-compute?embedded-checkout=true">sell excess compute</a> capacity, reports Bloomberg.</li>
<li>China’s <strong>ai</strong> released an <a href="https://counterpointresearch.com/en/insights/glm-5-2-challenges-anthropic-and-open-ai">open-source AI model</a>, GLM-5.2, that closes the performance gap on US frontier models, per Counterpoint.</li>
<li><strong>CoreWeave</strong> launched an <a href="https://www.coreweave.com/news/coreweave-aria-launches-as-an-ai-research-and-iteration-agent-with-autonomous-research-and-collaborative-intelligence">AI research and iteration agent</a>, ARIA, with autonomous research capabilities and collaborative intelligence.</li>
</ul>

<hr />
<h2 id="Reports and Deals"><strong>Reports and Deals</strong></h2>
<h3><strong>More deals, funding</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Infineon</strong> completed its <a href="https://www.infineon.com/press-release/2026/infxx202607-113">acquisition</a> of <strong>ams OSRAM’s</strong> non-optical analog/mixed-signal sensor portfolio, adding position, capacitive, temperature, medical imaging, and sensor-interface products.</li>
<li><strong>Rebellions</strong> acquired <strong>SqueezeBits</strong>, an <a href="https://rebellions.ai/newsroom/rebellions_squeezebits_acquisition_260630/">AI inference</a> specialist.</li>
<li><strong>SiTime</strong> <a href="https://www.sitime.com/company/newsroom/press-release/sitime-completes-acquisition-renesas-timing-business">completed</a> its previously announced acquisition of <strong>Renesas</strong>’ timing business.</li>
<li><strong>Nexchip Semiconductor</strong> <a href="https://static.sse.com.cn/disclosure/listedinfo/announcement/c/new/2026-06-30/688249_20260630_2Q37.pdf">filed details</a> for its Hong Kong H-share offering for R&amp;D and manufacturing build-out for its 22nm production line.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Reports</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li>Top Supply Chain <a href="https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-06-30-gartner-identifies-top-supply-chain-technology-trends-for-2026">Tech Trends</a> 2026 (<strong>Gartner</strong>)</li>
<li>Apple’s Across-the-Board Price<a href="https://www.trendforce.com/presscenter/news/20260701-13130.html"> Increases Add Uncertainty</a> (<strong>TF</strong>)</li>
<li>Semiconductors: Etching the <a href="https://www.mckinsey.com/mgi/our-research/semiconductors-etching-the-new-map-of-strategic-supply">new map of strategic supply</a> (<strong>McKinsey</strong>)</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Opinions</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://cepa.org/article/end-european-tech-pessimism/">End European Tech Pessimism</a> (<strong>CEPA</strong>)</li>
<li>The <a href="https://www.economist.com/leaders/2026/06/25/the-ai-backlash-is-only-getting-started">AI backlash</a> is only getting started (<strong>Economist</strong>)</li>
<li><a href="https://www.institude.org/opinion/europe-is-desperate-for-chips-but-the-outlook-doesnt-look-promising">Europe Is Desperate for Chips</a> (<strong>instituDE)</strong></li>
<li>Assessment of <a href="https://www.un.org/independent-international-scientific-panel-ai/en/preliminary-report">opportunities, risks and impacts of AI</a> (<strong>UN</strong>)</li>
<li>Evidence Contradicts <a href="https://itif.org/publications/2026/06/30/new-evidence-contradicts-myth-that-ai-is-destroying-jobs/">Myth that AI Is Destroying Jobs</a> (<strong>ITIF</strong>)</li>
<li><strong>Palantir</strong>’s Karp said something has <a href="https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/01/palantir-karp-open-ai-anthropic-tokens.html">gone wrong with the token model</a> (<strong>CNBC</strong>)</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2 id="New Technologies"><strong>New Technologies</strong></h2>
<p><strong>Keysight</strong> and <strong>Win Semiconductors</strong> released a joint <a href="https://www.keysight.com/us/en/about/newsroom/news-releases/2026/0630_pr25-076-keysight-and-win-semiconductors-collaborate-to-cut-design-risk-for-high-frequency-rf-components.html">MMIC design workflow</a> that connects on-chip multi-domain simulation, 3D layout with verification, and off-chip MMIC evaluation board design, into a single environment, enabling GaN MMIC design houses to achieve first pass tapeout success. The workflow is targeted at 5G base stations, Wi-Fi access points, satellite payloads, and defense radar systems.</p>
<p><strong>Toshiba</strong> launched an <a href="https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260629095995/en/Toshiba-Launches-80V-N-Channel-Power-MOSFET-Using-Its-Latest-Generation-Process-to-Improve-Efficiency-in-AI-Data-Centers">80V N-channel power MOSFET</a> fabricated using its latest generation process, U-MOS11-H, targeting switched-mode power supplies for industrial equipment used in AI data centers and communications base stations.</p>
<p><strong>GigaDevice</strong> launched its new GD24CL series I²C EEPROM of <a href="https://www.gigadevice.com/about/news-and-event/news/gigadevice-launches-i2c-eeprom">non-volatile memory</a> with up to 4 million program/erase cycles, integrated hardware-level error correction code (ECC) functionality, and an operating temperature range from -40°C to 125°C.</p>
<p><strong>Wave Photonics</strong> said all PDKs developed or managed through its PDK Management Platform are <a href="https://www.wavephotonics.com/news/wave-photonics-announces-synopsys-optocompiler-compatibility-for-pdks-hosted-on-its-pdk-management-platform">now compatible</a> with <strong>Synopsys</strong>&#8216; OptoCompiler, giving photonic IC designers access to PDKs across multiple materials and wavelengths, from blue to telecom bands.</p>
<hr />
<h2 id="Research"><strong>Research</strong></h2>
<p><strong>KAIST</strong>-led researchers demonstrated a monolithic <a href="https://advanced.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/adma.202523703?af=R">3D-integrated, all-solid ion-gated carbon nanotube transistor</a> that can tune ionic conductance for multi-timescale reservoir computing, pointing to more compact neuromorphic hardware for temporal data processing.</p>
<p>A <strong>Princeton</strong> team reported a method for making large-area <a href="https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.aee1510">2D semiconductors photo-programmable</a> with chromic molecular layers, allowing light to modify the material’s electronic properties after fabrication. The research opened a path toward reconfigurable semiconductor devices.</p>
<p>An <strong>MIT</strong> team modeled how the <a href="https://news.mit.edu/2026/how-data-centers-can-better-manage-energy-use-0626">AI data-center buildout</a> could affect the grid, finding that flexibility matters more than raw electricity use.</p>
<p><strong>NIST</strong>, <strong>U. of Colorado, Cornell</strong>, and other researchers achieved “impossible” <a href="https://news.cornell.edu/stories/2026/06/researchers-achieve-impossible-low-loss-tunable-dielectric">low-loss, tunable dielectric</a>, with potential for components used in wireless communications, radar systems, satellites and other devices that rely on controlling microwave signals with precision.</p>
<h3><strong>More research</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li>PIM and CPU <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30553">Execution on Mobile</a> Devices (<strong>SJTU, ETH </strong>et al.)</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00055">Hypergraph Partitioning</a> via Spectral Projection Optimization (<strong>Nvidia</strong>)</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.21430">Holographic EUV Lithography</a> at 40 nm Resolution (<strong>CAS, Paul Scherrer</strong> et al.)</li>
<li>Agentic <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27350">AI-driven HW/SW co-design</a> research (<strong>UC Berkeley</strong>)</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26701">Accelerating Sparse GEMM</a> w/Fine-Grained Dynamic Dataflow (<strong>UCLA</strong>)</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30949">Agentic Workflow for HLS</a> Compatibility &amp; Performance (<strong>Carnegie Mellon, UCLA</strong>)</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2 id="Security"><strong>Security</strong></h2>
<h3><strong>Government</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li>The Trump administration said it could <a href="https://cyberscoop.com/russell-vought-cisa-staffing-trump-budget-cuts/">add 600 new CISA staffers</a> after extensive budget cuts at the agency early last year.</li>
<li>The U.S. <a href="https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2072106151890809341">lifted its export controls</a> on <strong>Anthropic</strong>’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The reversal comes after the company added increased security measures to the models.</li>
<li><strong>NIST</strong> released updated guidance for developing security, privacy, and <a href="https://csrc.nist.gov/News/2026/nist-releases-sp-800-18r2">cybersecurity supply-chain risk-management</a> plans for systems.</li>
<li><strong>CISA</strong> and the <strong>DHS</strong> announced a new advisory council called <a href="https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/news/cisa-announces-new-advisory-council-strengthen-partnerships-and-secure-critical-infrastructure">ANCHOR-CI</a> that will oversee critical cybersecurity infrastructure in the U.S.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Threats</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>NVIDIA</strong> released <a href="https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/product-security/">five new critical/high-level security bulletins</a>, including updates addressing vulnerabilities in its AIStore Framework and Megatron Bridge products.</li>
<li><strong>Apple</strong> released <a href="https://support.apple.com/en-us/127594">OS, iPadOS, and macOS 26.5.2 updates</a> ahead of schedule <a href="https://www.reuters.com/business/apple-says-it-is-releasing-updates-early-response-ai-cybersecurity-concerns-2026-06-29/">amid AI-driven cybersecurity concerns.</a></li>
<li>Data Fusion: As AI systems grow more powerful and pervasive, they are transforming data into increasingly precise maps of people’s lives. By <a href="https://semiengineering.com/defending-against-ai-enabled-data-fusion/">fusing data from various sources</a>, attackers can construct highly detailed digital twins of individuals and their environments, making it easier to attack specific targets.</li>
<li><strong>CISA</strong> alerts for the week are <a href="https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories">here</a>.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Security collaborations</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Fraunhofer&#8217;s</strong> <strong>AISEC</strong> and<strong> IOSB</strong> joined Germany&#8217;s Semiconductor-X project as cybersecurity partners, developing security roadmaps, gateway specifications, and governance concepts for <a href="https://www.iosb.fraunhofer.de/en/press/press-releases/2026/fraunhofer-aisec-and-iosb-are-security-partners-for--secure-data.html">secure data exchange</a> across semiconductor manufacturing supply chains.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Cybersecurity papers</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31681">Side-Channel Protections</a> in HW Implementations of PQC ML-KEM Verification</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00186">Side-Channel IP Theft Attack</a> on Additive Manufacturing Using Dual Smartphones</li>
<li>High-Performance <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.00621">NTT Accelerators for PQC</a> (<strong>DUTH, Nokia</strong>)</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30783">The Hidden Cost of Prompt Injection Defense</a> (<strong>UIUC, Amazon</strong>)</li>
<li>CPO Enabled Silicon Photonics <a href="https://semiengineering.com/photonic-crystal-fingerprints-target-silicon-photonics-supply-chain-security/">Security Assurance HW Fingerprinting</a> (<strong>UF</strong>)</li>
<li>Nanoelectromechanical Systems for <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26426">HW Security in Advanced Packaging</a> (<strong>UF)</strong></li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30755">Claw-like Agent Security </a>Through a Computer-Systems Lens (<strong>UIUC, Berkeley, et al.</strong>)</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2 id="Vehicles, Batteries"><strong>Vehicles, Batteries</strong></h2>
<h3><strong>Business moves</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li>Swiss semiconductor developer <strong>SEALSQ</strong> announced plans to expand its business into <a href="https://www.sealsq.com/investors/news-releases/sealsq-to-bring-post-quantum-security-to-the-automotive-industry">automotive cybersecurity,</a> positioning itself as a supplier of post-quantum automotive chips.</li>
<li><strong>Ferrari</strong> and <strong>BMW</strong> both introduced new vehicle models <a href="https://www.reuters.com/business/autos-transportation/why-companies-are-swapping-copper-aluminium-2026-06-30/">built with aluminum wiring</a> amid an ongoing spike in copper prices.</li>
<li>Israeli automotive security firm <strong>PlaxidityX </strong>(aka Argus), <a href="https://www.haaretz.com/israel-news/business/2026-07-01/ty-article/.premium/israeli-automotive-cybersecurity-firm-plaxidityx-shuts-down-after-450m-exit/0000019f-1cf5-da71-abdf-5dfd6d670000">will shut down,</a> 9 years after it was acquired for $450M in 2017.</li>
<li><strong>Siemens</strong> announced an expansion of its partnership <a href="https://news.siemens.com/en-us/siemens-luna-rossa/">with yacht racing team Luna Rossa</a>, supporting the sailing team with systems engineering, structural optimization, product design and traceability within an integrated digital engineering environment. Motorsports engineering firm <strong>ORECA Group</strong> selected <strong>Siemens’</strong><a href="https://news.siemens.com/en-us/siemens-simcenter-oreca-group/"> Simcenter software</a> to support advanced CFD and composite structural optimization for its high-performance racing vehicle development programs.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>EVs</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>McKinsey</strong> identified platform <a href="https://www.mckinsey.com/mgi/our-research/automotive-r-and-d-charging-ahead-in-ev-platform-development">research and development time</a> as a key determiner of EV brand success as consumer interest in electric driving grows.</li>
<li>Leading Indian EV maker <strong>Tata Motors</strong> <a href="https://ev.tatamotors.com/sierra/ev.html">debuted the Sierra EV</a>, a new electric SUV with QWD.</li>
<li>Chinese EV startup <strong>XPENG</strong> debuted a <a href="https://www.xpeng.com/pressroom/news/019f12539bff9f1220b48a028223000e">new technical framework for autonomous driving</a> called XMIND, built with an embedded World Model for efficient visual Chain-of-Thought.</li>
<li>The EV revolution relies on battery innovation, while AI data centers need a range of <a href="https://semiengineering.com/ai-data-centers-and-auto-industry-converge-on-same-issues/">new energy solutions</a> to play nicely with the grid. Both sectors are taking notes.</li>
<li><strong>The European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association</strong> came out in support of EU leaders’ <a href="https://www.acea.auto/press-release/acea-publishes-position-on-the-industrial-accelerator-act/">proposed Industrial Accelerator Act</a>, which would provide benefits to European carmakers and set timelines for EV battery production.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Autonomous, drones, connectivity</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Waymo</strong> and<strong> Uber</strong> <a href="https://www.automotiveworld.com/news/waymo-and-uber-end-phoenix-service-as-tensions-deepen/">quietly ended</a> their collaboration in Phoenix, putting an end to robotaxi service in the city.</li>
<li>The UK government will invest <a href="https://www.gov.uk/government/news/uk-drone-transformation-to-strengthen-armed-forces-backed-by-more-than-5-billion">$5B in military drone technology,</a> including investment in the recently opened Uncrewed Systems Centre, Europe’s largest drone testing facility. This commitment is the largest investment in drone development in the country’s history.</li>
<li>Law firm<strong> Holland &amp; Knight</strong> released an analysis on how developing connected vehicle regulations are set to affect <a href="https://www.hklaw.com/en/insights/publications/2026/07/connected-vehicles-face-growing-national-security-scrutiny">automakers, suppliers and technology providers</a>.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Vehicle technical papers</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/html/2606.30564v1">Role of Vehicles</a> in Digital Forensic Investigations (<strong>TH Rosenheim</strong>)</li>
<li>Repair-before-veto control for <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27781">safe lithium-ion fast charging </a> (<strong>McGill</strong>)</li>
<li>Radar-Camera Fusion for Long-Range<a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31096"> 3D Perception in AD</a> (<strong>SNU</strong>)</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2 id="Trending Video"><strong>Trending Video</strong></h2>
<p><a href="https://semiengineering.com/will-your-chips-memory-work-as-expected/">Will Your Chip’s Memory Work As Expected</a>?<br />
How to ensure that memory is fully tested and repaired.</p>
<p>Yervant Zorian, chief architect and fellow at <strong>Synopsys</strong>, explains why a hierarchical approach is now essential for testing memory, what can go wrong while testing different memories tied to different types of processors, and why massive multi-physics simulations and digital twins are needed for everything from smartphones to robots.</p>
<p><iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/QxU09Tkl_Mg" width="480" height="270" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen"></iframe></p>
<hr />
<h2 id="Quantum"><strong>Quantum</strong></h2>
<p>The U.S. <strong>NSA</strong> and the <strong>DEVCOM</strong>  launched a <a href="https://www.nsa.gov/Press-Room/Press-Releases-Statements/Press-Release-View/Article/4529557/nsa-devcom-army-research-office-launch-quantumeagle-initiative/">QuantumEAGLe Initiative</a>, focusing on five areas of the U.S. quantum industry: industry engagement, commercial roadmaps, supply chain advancement, algorithmic applications, and foundational research.</p>
<p><strong>Microsoft </strong>is accelerating the timeline for its Quantum Safe Program with the goal of <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/06/30/microsoft-advances-quantum-safe-security-as-the-risk-timeline-shifts/">transitioning all products and services to PQC by 2029.</a> The move follows French and American government orders setting deadlines for PQC compliance for critical infrastructure operators in 2030.</p>
<h3><strong>QC funding</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Quantum Global Tech</strong> <a href="https://gov.texas.gov/news/post/governor-abbott-announces-texas-semiconductor-innovation-fund-grant-to-quantum-global-technologies">won</a> a Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund grant of $3M for its new Austin services facility.</li>
<li><strong>SEEQC </strong><a href="https://seeqc.com/resources/seeqc-files-registration-statement-for-proposed-initial-public-offering">filed</a> for a proposed IPO.</li>
<li><strong>Qolab</strong> raised $54M towards its <a href="https://qolab.ai/press-releases/qolab-announces-54-2-million-series-b-financing-and-commitments-led-by-uc-investments">scalable quantum HW systems</a>.</li>
<li><strong>D-Wave</strong> <a href="https://www.dwavequantum.com/company/newsroom/press-release/d-wave-to-receive-1-5-million-grant-through-nsf-project/">won</a> a $1.5M grant from the U.S. government to support its role in the ERASE (Erasure Qubits and Dynamic Circuits for Quantum Advantage) project, focused on foundational technologies for fault-tolerant quantum computing.</li>
</ul>
<h3><strong>Quantum Research</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>U. of Sydney</strong> and <strong>IBM</strong> researchers identified and quantified important factors <a href="https://www.sydney.edu.au/news-opinion/news/2026/06/23/pathway-to-high-fidelity-quantum-computing-identified-in-new-res.html">limiting the performance of quantum</a>.</li>
<li><strong>ETH Zurich</strong> researchers used a single ion to <a href="https://ethz.ch/en/news-and-events/eth-news/news/2026/07/3d-scanner-for-electromagnetic-fields.html">detect electromagnetic fields</a> above a surface and to create a 3D map of them, with potential to improve chips for quantum computers and quantum sensors.</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2 id="Workforce, Education"><strong>Workforce, Education</strong></h2>
<p><strong>Micron</strong> announced a $250M investment to increase long-term savings opportunities for <a href="https://investors.micron.com/news-releases/news-release-details/micron-announces-250-million-investment-trump-accounts-reaching">children and families</a> through the U.S.&#8217;s new 530A accounts, with employee matching benefits.</p>
<p><strong>Business Oregon</strong> released a statewide <a href="https://www.oregon.gov/biz/aboutus/blog/Pages/Oregon_CHIPS_at_a_Crossroads.aspx">semiconductor ecosystem assessment</a> warning that Oregon’s long-established semiconductor cluster remains strong but is increasingly vulnerable as other states compete more aggressively for fab, supplier, and advanced-packaging investments.</p>
<p>Bloomberg reports that <a href="https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-26/ai-anxiety-is-fueling-burnout-across-silicon-valley-s-tech-workers">AI is increasing burnout</a> across Silicon Valley, with some working 16-hour days, sleeping at the office, and running multiple AI agents around the clock as faster development cycles raise expectations rather than ease workloads.</p>
<p><strong>Vietnam</strong> and <strong>Germany</strong> plan to <a href="https://vir.com.vn/vietnam-and-germany-expand-cooperation-in-semiconductors-and-high-tech-manpower-155886.html">expand cooperation</a> on semiconductors and high-tech workforce development.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.economist.com/science-and-technology/2026/06/24/why-big-ai-labs-are-hiring-so-many-philosophers">Hot new jobs for the AI era</a>: philosophers, reports the Economist.</p>
<h3><strong>New education offerings</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Syracuse U</strong>. and <strong>Cornell</strong> are offering a free <a href="https://news.syr.edu/2026/06/30/free-nsf-i-corps-course-to-be-offered-this-fall/">course this fall</a> for researchers working on semiconductors or advanced materials. Apply by August 26.</li>
<li>The <strong>U. of Washington</strong> opened applications for its July 28-August 1 <a href="https://sites.uw.edu/ceadvice/2026/06/30/upwards-nanofabrication-summer-camp-applications-now-open/?">Nanofabrication Summer Camp </a>that will give students hands-on semiconductor fabrication lab training, lectures, and exposure to nanofabrication technologies. Application deadline is July 17.</li>
<li>The <strong>U. of Wisconsin–Madison</strong> opened a new <a href="https://news.wisc.edu/the-future-is-now-college-of-computing-artificial-intelligence-officially-launches/">College of Computing &amp; AI</a>.</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2 id="Events and Webinars"><strong>Events and Webinars</strong></h2>
<p>Upcoming webinars are <a href="https://semiengineering.com/webinars/" aria-label="Upcoming webinars are here , including:" data-uw-rm-vglnk="" data-uw-rm-brl="PR" data-uw-original-href="https://semiengineering.com/webinars/">here</a>, including:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://www.infineon.com/event/webinar/2026/AURIX-TC49x-microcontroller-xEV-integration-platforms">High-performance MCU</a> for xEV-integration platforms, July 8</li>
<li>Build Your <a class="e-title" href="https://riscure.ac-page.com/build-your-device-security-lab" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" data-uw-rm-brl="PR" data-uw-original-href="https://riscure.ac-page.com/build-your-device-security-lab" aria-label="Build Your Device Security Lab: From Threats to Test Capabilities​ - open in a new tab" data-uw-rm-ext-link="">Device Security Lab</a>, July 9</li>
<li>AI Driving the <a class="e-title" href="https://discover.semi.org/ai-driving-the-semiconductor-supercycle-webinar-registration.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" data-uw-rm-brl="PR" data-uw-original-href="https://discover.semi.org/ai-driving-the-semiconductor-supercycle-webinar-registration.html" aria-label="AI Driving the Semiconductor Supercycle—Rising AI Demand and the Semiconductor Innovation Loop - open in a new tab" data-uw-rm-ext-link="">Semiconductor Supercycle</a>, July 9</li>
<li>Scaling Compute Connectivity with <a class="e-title" href="https://www.synopsys.com/webinars/compute-connectivity-pcie-cxl-architectures.html?utm_source=semiengineering&amp;utm_medium=link&amp;utm_campaign=hpcip&amp;utm_content=compute-connectivity-pcie-cxl-wbr_link_mul_wbn&amp;tags=Webinars" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" data-uw-rm-brl="PR" data-uw-original-href="https://www.synopsys.com/webinars/compute-connectivity-pcie-cxl-architectures.html?utm_source=semiengineering&amp;utm_medium=link&amp;utm_campaign=hpcip&amp;utm_content=compute-connectivity-pcie-cxl-wbr_link_mul_wbn&amp;tags=Webinars" aria-label="Scaling Compute Connectivity with PCIe and CXL: Chip-to-Chip and Emerging Architectures - open in a new tab" data-uw-rm-ext-link="">PCIe and CXL</a>, July 14</li>
<li><a class="e-title" href="https://cadencedesign.registration.goldcast.io/webinar/af23bedd-33b1-4f3b-9af2-6caf2991d262/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" data-uw-rm-brl="PR" data-uw-original-href="https://cadencedesign.registration.goldcast.io/webinar/af23bedd-33b1-4f3b-9af2-6caf2991d262/" aria-label="What EMC Failures Really Cost Engineering Teams - open in a new tab" data-uw-rm-ext-link="">What EMC Failures </a>Really Cost Engineering Teams, July 15</li>
</ul>

					<description><![CDATA[<p>The EV revolution relies on battery innovation, while AI data centers need a range of new energy solutions to play nice with the grid. Both sectors are taking notes.</p>
<p>The post <a href="https://semiengineering.com/ai-data-centers-and-auto-industry-converge-on-same-issues/">AI Data Centers And Auto Industry Converge On Same Issues</a> appeared first on <a href="https://semiengineering.com">Semiconductor Engineering</a>.</p>
]]></description>
										<content:encoded><![CDATA[<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Key Takeaways</span></b><span data-contrast="auto">: </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="1" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">AI data centers need power from a range of sources, including batteries, to safeguard against blackouts, transient voltage spikes, and grid demand spikes. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="1" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">As with regenerative braking and bidirectional charging in electric vehicles, data centers could feed power or heat back into the grid for public use, but the immediate goal is to disrupt the grid as little as possible.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="1" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">Overseeing battery and energy storage systems is a multi-tiered software management layer that monitors everything from the battery cells to the interaction between the data center and the grid.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<hr />
<p><span data-contrast="auto">AI data centers and automotive companies are converging on the same energy-related problems from different angles, but they are leveraging technology improvements developed by each other to accelerate the timetable for solutions.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Both share a common goal of obtaining more energy. For AI data centers, this is a function of insatiable demand for compute power, which is needed to shorten time to results with greater accuracy. For smart cars, which increasingly resemble data centers on wheels, it&#8217;s a matter of adding more compute-intensive intelligence and, in the case of EVs, improved range per charge.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Yet both are running into the same limitations. Power grid capacity isn&#8217;t increasing nearly as fast as demand for energy, and the technology needed to significantly improve efficiency takes time to develop. At the same time, each is encountering increased costs and political pressure stemming from rising utility rates. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">The solution comes in three parts: </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="3" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">Increasing battery storage, with bi-directional charging and sophisticated energy management, to ensure there is sufficient power available for peak loads;</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="3" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">Different materials and chip architectures that can handle higher voltages, allowing for more compute density and less data movement, and</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="3" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">More alternative energy sources to maintain power grid neutrality.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Bi-directional charging is available in some vehicle models today, and it has been gaining traction as an inexpensive way of storing excess energy collected by solar panels and wind farms. Early battery storage farms utilize batteries that are considered end-of-life for vehicles because they have insufficient capacity for BEV range, but they are still useful for other applications. On top of that, battery prices have been dropping due to increased competition and new battery chemistries, which are driving new battery sales.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“As batteries become much less expensive, the growth of battery energy storage systems (BESS) will continue in a big way,” observed Peter Wawer, division president of green industrial power at </span><a href="https://semiengineering.com/entities/infineon-technologies/"><span data-contrast="auto">Infineon</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">. “It&#8217;s estimated that data centers will, at a point in the future, overtake total consumption compared to the automotive market.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">An ESS, comprised of thousands of batteries managed by a battery management system, can be used as backup if the grid goes out or even voltage fluctuations, and as the main power source during peak grid times.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“AI data centers are a different beast than typical data centers,” said </span><span data-contrast="none">Sinha Puneet, senior director and global head of battery at </span><a href="https://semiengineering.com/entities/mentor-a-siemens-business/"><span data-contrast="none">Siemens EDA</span></a><span data-contrast="none">. </span><span data-contrast="auto">“There are two key differentiators between a normal data center, which has been there for the last 15-plus years, versus the new AI data centers. AI data centers require a lot more joules, a lot more energy, because AI jobs are a lot more energy-consuming. Also, there is more fluctuation in energy demand for AI data centers — approximately 10X more fluctuations in energy demand compared to a regular data center — because as one AI job finishes, another comes in. If not managed correctly, fluctuation can brown out the whole grid. That&#8217;s why energy storage is extremely central for any AI data center’s operation, and that’s why you see all this massive new demand for better energy storage systems to provide that energy.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">To manage those fluctuations, fast-acting battery energy storage becomes critical, particularly if there is any disruption on the utility side. “ESS can provide energy for a few hours, but absorbing that fluctuation and providing the energy is extremely critical because these AI data centers require a lot of energy,” said Sinha. “In the U.S., you have a peak-time energy rate, which is very high. Increasingly, companies are looking at ESS to reduce their OpEx, especially the energy rate for them. They want to take away that peak-time energy consumption to batteries. A lot of normal battery energy storage systems today provide approximately one to four hours of operation. But companies are also looking at long-duration energy storage systems, such as what Google is trying to do with </span><a href="https://formenergy.com/"><span data-contrast="none">Form Energy</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, the flow battery company.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Flow batteries are rechargeable energy management systems that store chemical energy in liquid electrolytes contained in external tanks. In the future, a longer-duration ESS will be essential to address another challenge, which is the difficulty and time-consuming process of connecting AI data centers to the grid. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“Grid connection is an extremely time-consuming process,” said Sinha. “The last I checked, you&#8217;re looking at a few years in many cases to have that connection, which is why many companies are talking about having on-site gas generators and other power options, because it takes so long for all the compliance and whatnot to obtain the grid connection. Behind the meter, companies are looking at whether they can have on-site energy generation, as well as exploring the use of long-duration energy sources from flow batteries and other technologies that can give them longer duration operation without waiting for the grid connection.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Managing energy movement, particularly with a mixture of equipment, is non-trivial. “How do you move a lot of energy safely, efficiently, and intelligently between the grid, storage, and highly dynamic loads?&#8221; asked Hoa Tram, senior principal product engineer at </span><a href="https://semiengineering.com/entities/cadence-design-systems/">Cadence</a><span data-contrast="none">. “EVs brought us high</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">voltage battery packs, sophisticated BMS, and bidirectional power electronics for regenerative braking and vehicle</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">to</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">grid, governed by strict functional</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">safety processes — and those ideas are now showing up in data centers through large</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">scale battery energy</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">storage systems, layered battery management, and smarter interaction with the grid.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><strong>Sharing technology </strong><br />
<span data-contrast="none">What&#8217;s important to note here is the flow of ideas is not a one</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">way street. </span><span data-contrast="auto">Engineers across the electric vehicle and data center sectors are now exchanging ideas on how to optimize energy and battery use, and potentially feed excess power back to the grid in a symbiotic relationship.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="none">“Hyperscale data centers are leaders in using telemetry, analytics, and digital twins to plan and operate complex infrastructure, and automotive OEMs and suppliers are adopting similar approaches for software</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">defined vehicles, fleet</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">level battery analytics, and power/thermal optimization,” Tram noted. “Cadence sits in the middle of that cross</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">pollination — the same system</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">level modeling, functional</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">safety flows, and data</span>‑<span data-contrast="none">center digital twins we provide for hyperscale customers are being used to design the next generation of automotive and energy systems, as well.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">On the flip side, some vehicles already return excess power via bidirectional energy systems, including vehicle-to-grid (V2G), vehicle-to-home (V2H), and vehicle-to-load (V2L). </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“As vehicles become electric and connected, they become a moving energy asset on the road,” said Negar Soufi Amlashi, senior vice president of sales at </span><span data-contrast="auto">Infineon</span><span data-contrast="auto">. “They not only consume power, but they generate it, store it, and even feed it back to the grid. This symbiotic relationship becomes pivotal. This is a transformation, powered by semiconductors and software, enabling clean propulsion and efficient connectivity between vehicle and grid. Every single watt saved translates to value created.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Devices at play here include bidirectional traction inverters, bidirectional on-board chargers, power modules, and solid-state charging stations, based on silicon, SiC, and GaN. “This enables a power conversion which is very efficient, helps reduce losses, and makes efficiency the new currency,” Amlashi noted.  </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">AI-driven energy management makes the vehicle an essential part of the grid that can optimize power in real time. “The battery duration can be expanded by optimizing the usage of energy, which extends the range of the electric car,” said Amlashi. “Further, we enable an efficient integration of the vehicle to the grid, for example, by efficient bidirectional charging.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">With that in mind, many are asking whether data centers could achieve the same optimizations through battery energy storage systems, alternative sources of on-site power, or micro-DC grids.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“DC grids are already creeping in from the industrial side,” said </span><span data-contrast="auto">Infineon</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Wawer. “Quite a few companies are equipping typical industrial manufacturing sites with DC grids, because the logic is very simple. You can connect consumers and gain battery storage through this kind of system, while massively increasing the efficiency. For example, today, industrial robots typically use resistors for braking. If you&#8217;re moving the arm and you have to slow it down, you brake, and you consume the energy via a resistor, which is totally inefficient. With the DC grid, you can change the topology, remove the resistor, and instead break by generation and feed the energy back into the grid — convert it like with the EV. If you break, it&#8217;s converted and fed into the battery. It opens up a huge variety of topics, including DC-fed generation, because you have some solar panels on the roof or close by. These things are already moving, slowly but steadily, in typical industrial style. That is massively being accelerated due to the AI data center guys jumping on it, so it nicely fits together.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">New materials and architectures</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"><br />
<span data-contrast="auto">Cranking up the voltage in systems, rather than increasing the current, is another piece of the solution. That enables greater power density per given area, and less data movement, which is expensive in terms of resources and dollars. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Together, the semiconductor, automotive, and energy industries are exploring solutions at every layer of the stack so that cost per token remains affordable as the AI revolution continues at pace. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“We get a lot of push on density,” said Kristof Beets, vice president of product management at </span><a href="https://semiengineering.com/entities/imagination-technologies/">Imagination Technologies</a><span data-contrast="auto">. “There is a push for getting as much out of the physical space, as well as the power budgets, that data centers and automotive have. But most of the push we get is very much from the efficiency point of view. That tends to be the other markets more than the server space — the server space is still a secondary consideration.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">However, this trend may soon reverse as power needs grow, and AI inference costs rise with a focus on intelligence per watt. That will require different architectures and materials.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">According to a recent Morgan Stanley report on energy markets [1], “&#8230;investors noted that power suppliers and power equipment companies are likely to see benefits from data-center expansion, particularly among expectations that AI will unleash a productivity wave throughout the broader economy. This has investors focused on the rising demand for off-grid solutions, eliminating bottlenecks in the energy supply chain and using credit markets to finance energy system growth. While most sites also maintain their ties to the grid, investors expect to see more developers shift toward hybrid or off-grid models that let developers ensure the resilience of their operations in a tightening global power market, which places a higher focus on power equipment providers.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">That, in turn, requires new materials. “We are talking about power rails in the several thousands of amperes,” said Helmut Puchner, vice president and fellow, Aerospace &amp; Defense, at </span>Infineon Technologies<span data-contrast="auto">. “This is scaling massively. The old power supplies were single-phase power supplies. You switch your transistor on and off. Now we are talking 12 or 16 phases. Gate drivers can handle 150 amps each, or more. SiC (silicon carbide) can cover more than 1,000V. GaN (gallium nitride) is in the medium-range voltage, and silicon can cover from 650 volts down to 10 volts.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Wide bandgap materials such as SiC and GaN are enabling data centers to shift from a 48 V DC rack-level power architecture to 800V. “This is a big jump,” said Pradeep Shenoy, compute power technologist at </span>Texas Instruments<span data-contrast="auto">. “I liken it to the electric vehicle market, or vehicles in general. Today, you might have a 12V lead-acid battery. Some vehicles have 48V systems. If you look at EVs on the market today, they have batteries that are 400V or 800V. We&#8217;ve been working on that technology for quite a long time, so we&#8217;re able to quickly leverage a lot of technology around isolation or gallium nitride that was developed for other markets and apply it to data centers. Some of the architects and key leaders in the data center space will say they’re trying to borrow technology that’s already been developed for the electric vehicle market. 800V-based EVs are very common. They&#8217;re on the road today, and the infrastructure can be leveraged quickly.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Data center vs. automotive battery management</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span><br />
Overseeing battery energy storage systems and energy management between the data center, grid, and renewable power sources is a hierarchy of AI-enabled software systems, including battery management systems (BMS), energy management systems (EMS), and grid-interactive UPS systems (GiUPS).<span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“There&#8217;s some level of energy management that&#8217;s happening at the data center, but there&#8217;s also going to be some level of coordination and communication with the power grid,” said </span><span data-contrast="auto">TI</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Shenoy. “At a high level, it will be the data center and the grid interacting. But within a data center, there will be systems controlling how much power they pull from the grid, how much power they pull from local battery storage, or store power into it. There will be management at different levels.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">In both automotive and data center applications, the </span><span data-contrast="none">BMS monitors thermal input, hotspots, coolants, electrical controls, overcharging, or over-discharging. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="none">“The thermal hydraulic cooling loop is often the most challenging,” noted </span><span data-contrast="auto">Bryan Kelly, principal engineer at </span><a href="https://semiengineering.com/entities/synopsys-inc/">Synopsys</a><span data-contrast="auto">.</span><span data-contrast="none"> “This area of mechatronics typically falls outside the hands-on expertise of many hardware/software battery pack design engineers. Although CFD software tools can support preliminary ‘what if’ thermal analyses, ultimately a virtual prototype of the complete cooling system — cooling plate, piping, hoses, manifolds, and related components — is essential.” </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Such a model enables verification across different coolant types or mixture ratios, environmental conditions, and operating scenarios, and allows results to be validated against measurement data. “In addition, an end-to-end thermal story of the battery pack simulation model makes it possible to study required heating at extremely low temperatures, evaluate HW/SW control behavior across wide temperature ranges, and simulate fault conditions — such as reduced coolant flow during high load current demand — that are difficult or impractical to reproduce on a physical test bench,” said Kelly.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Alternative and renewable sources of power</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span><br />
The last piece of the three-part solution involves a variety of energy sources to maintain constant, uninterruptible data center power. <span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“The only solution is to have an appropriate mix of power sources, meaning solar and wind. On shorter notice, it&#8217;s most likely gas turbines that are being made available,” said </span><span data-contrast="auto">Infineon</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Wawer. “All other forms, especially if we think about nuclear, take much too long [to build]. If I need X, Y, Z terawatt hours, and you start building a nuclear power plant, let&#8217;s see when this will be ready. The requirements are much shorter term, and the short-term availability of energy can only be supplied by renewables, plus the combination of energy storage.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Recommissioning existing nuclear plants is a faster solution. “I live in Pennsylvania, and they&#8217;re trying to reinstate the Three Mile Island nuclear power plant,” said Steven Lee, product manager of power electronics design software at </span><a href="https://semiengineering.com/entities/keysight-technologies/">Keysight Technologies</a><span data-contrast="auto">. “They’re recommissioning it to power AI data centers that are coming everywhere. They&#8217;re going to continue to use the grid for distribution, but are going to rely on nuclear power to satisfy the energy demands. Electricity for you and for me, at our houses, is going to become a lot more expensive as a result of AI data centers. That will be the immediate effect for us.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">If regions do not already have nuclear, deploying solar is easier in terms of permit time, availability of solar panels, and installation. “The cost per kilowatt hour has reached a point below one cent per kilowatt,” said Wawer. “That alone should drive solar installations to be a large part of the solution going forward, to provide additional electricity as fast as possible.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">The conversion power sequence to get from solar panel to battery to data center or grid is the reverse of that of grid to gate. “Solar panels create DC (using photovoltaic cells),” said Lee. “Then you&#8217;re trying to go the other way and convert it into AC using a solar inverter. That&#8217;s why it’s called an inverter, because it&#8217;s DC to AC for solar, whereas it’s an AC to DC flow to get from the electric grid to the data center processors. It’s related, but backwards.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">The solar inverter can be placed before or after a battery, or both, depending on whether the system is AC-coupled or DC-coupled. The battery holds the DC charge, and then the inverter converts it to AC energy for the grid. The energy is then converted back to DC again via, for example, a two-stage 800V DC power architecture, to be used by the data center rack processors, Lee explained. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><img data-recalc-dims="1" decoding="async" class="alignnone size-large wp-image-24278276" src="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Image.jpeg?resize=1024%2C652&#038;ssl=1" alt="" width="1024" height="652" srcset="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Image.jpeg?resize=1024%2C652&amp;ssl=1 1024w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Image.jpeg?resize=300%2C191&amp;ssl=1 300w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Image.jpeg?resize=768%2C489&amp;ssl=1 768w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Image.jpeg?resize=600%2C382&amp;ssl=1 600w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Image.jpeg?w=1320&amp;ssl=1 1320w" sizes="(max-width: 1000px) 100vw, 1000px" /></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Fig. 1: An EV charging from a wind and solar powered energy storage system. </span></b><a href="https://www.cadence.com/en_US/home/resources/technical-briefs/ev-efficiency-design-analysis.html"><b><span data-contrast="none">Source</span></b></a><b><span data-contrast="auto">: Cadence</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">EVs also leverage solar power, and are further along than data centers when it comes to potentially giving excess power back to the grid. For example, solar panels on the roof of a home can be connected to a Tesla wall unit. “It takes the DC solar power, charges the battery in that wall unit, then an inverter converts it to AC to charge the car or another appliance in your home,” said </span><span data-contrast="auto">Keysight</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Lee. “Also, if the grid requires electricity from your house, because it&#8217;s short, then the power can go backwards into the grid, and then the grid can distribute that to someone else&#8217;s house, or a data center.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">It&#8217;s the same chain happening, no matter which way the power is flowing. “There&#8217;s a battery in the middle, and then instead of power coming from the grid, you could be putting it back into the grid,” said Lee. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><img data-recalc-dims="1" loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignnone wp-image-24278277 size-large" src="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_0362.jpg?resize=1024%2C505&#038;ssl=1" alt="" width="1024" height="505" srcset="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_0362.jpg?resize=1024%2C505&amp;ssl=1 1024w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_0362.jpg?resize=300%2C148&amp;ssl=1 300w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_0362.jpg?resize=768%2C379&amp;ssl=1 768w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_0362.jpg?resize=600%2C296&amp;ssl=1 600w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_0362.jpg?w=1404&amp;ssl=1 1404w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 1000px) 100vw, 1000px" /></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Fig. 2: Challenges in modern power grids. Source: Infineon</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Other options</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span><br />
Similar to existing home and EV models, a data center with on-site energy generation could theoretically give excess energy back to the grid. “It depends on what sort of timescales we&#8217;re talking about,” said <span data-contrast="auto">TI</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Shenoy. “There are some microsecond-level spikes of power that a data center might have, in very short timescales, where the grid is usually operating on much lower, longer timescales, relatively speaking. Other concerns from the power grid perspective are, ‘What if this data center has fluctuations or disturbances and takes down the power grid?’ That would not be good, and there&#8217;s a lot of activity and engineering know-how focused on ensuring the data center operates in a way that plays nicely with the grid. Even if it isn&#8217;t necessarily sending power back to the grid, if it is behaving properly as a load to the grid, that would actually be a very good accomplishment.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">A simpler possibility is to feed excess heat from data centers back to local utilities companies, as seen recently in Scandinavia. [2] [3]</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“Data centers</span> <span data-contrast="auto">are just converting energy, electrical energy, into heat, like any power plant or heating plant,” said </span><span data-contrast="auto">Infineon</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Puchner. “I grew up in Austria, and for generations, we were burning garbage with extra fuel, and so you get rid of the garbage, and as a side product, you generate heat, which is distributed to 60,000 to 100,000 households. I can see that model being used, instead of heating the river by two or three degrees for data center cooling. Why don&#8217;t they give the heat back to the community? We can all use heat in winter.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Heating nearby facilities is technically challenging, but possible. The real issue is cost.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“The systems exist. The technology is there. But the investment might be too high for data center companies,” said Puchner. “If the general public or governments are willing to spend that, it&#8217;s a good idea. Those data centers run 24/7 and generate a lot of heat.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Repurposing innovations look set to grow, whether they are to take advantage of excess energy or heat, or to recycle old batteries and hardware. Two recent examples: </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="2" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">Waymo transformed retired EV batteries into clean energy storage for essential local infrastructure. [4]</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li aria-setsize="-1" data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="2" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;hybridMultilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="1"><span data-contrast="auto">UC San Diego and Google researchers extracted motherboards of retired smartphones, collected them into clusters, and redeployed them as a general-purpose computing platform for a data center to be used by researchers and students. [5]</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Conclusion: Why this matters</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span><br />
More efficient chip, network, and converter architectures are all part of the solution to help reduce AI data centers’ power consumption. The other side of the solution is to make use of cleaner, renewable sources of energy so that power used by AI data centers does not negatively impact the general population in terms of availability and cost. <span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Electric vehicles promise similar relief from reliance on limited oil supplies. However, it&#8217;s worth noting that depending on where a driver’s electric grid gets its power — from nuclear to hydro to coal — EVs are not necessarily a lot cleaner than gas-powered cars. “You are just looking at one very specific part of the timeline, which is my car, but you&#8217;re not looking upstream where the power comes from,” </span><span data-contrast="auto">Keysight</span><span data-contrast="auto">’s Lee noted. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">References</span></b><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">[1] Energy Markets Race to Solve the </span><a href="https://www.morganstanley.com/insights/articles/powering-ai-energy-market-outlook-2026"><span data-contrast="none">AI Power Bottleneck</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> (Morgan Stanley)</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">[2] Finland’s </span><a href="https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2025-05-14/finland-s-data-centers-are-heating-cities-too?embedded-checkout=true"><span data-contrast="none">Data Centers Are Heating Cities</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> Too (Bloomberg)</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">[3] DEN01 </span><a href="https://www.atnorth.com/news/denmark-data-center-to-supply-heat-to-danish-homes/"><span data-contrast="none">data center to supply heat</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> to Danish homes (atNorth)</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">[4] A </span><a href="https://waymo.com/blog/2026/06/b2u-partnership/"><span data-contrast="none">second life for EV batteries</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, clean energy for communities (Waymo)</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">[5] A low-carbon computing platform from your </span><a href="https://research.google/blog/a-low-carbon-computing-platform-from-your-retired-phones/"><span data-contrast="none">retired phones</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> (UC San Diego, Google)</span><span data-ccp-props="{}"> </span></p>

<p><span data-contrast="auto">New types of sensors can generate environmental data in real time using a range of tools, including flexible, printed ICs and AI/ML.</span></p>

		<item>
		<title>Defending Against AI-Enabled Data Fusion</title>
		<link>https://semiengineering.com/defending-against-ai-enabled-data-fusion/</link>
					<comments>https://semiengineering.com/defending-against-ai-enabled-data-fusion/#respond</comments>
		
		<dc:creator><![CDATA[Ann Mutschler]]></dc:creator>
		<pubDate>Thu, 02 Jul 2026 07:13:41 +0000</pubDate>
				<category><![CDATA[IoT and Security]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Top Stories]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[ARM]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[automotive security]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Cadence]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[data integrity]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[data synchronization]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Imagination]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[multimodal AI data fusion]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Rambus]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Root of Trust]]></category>
